 {"id":"gt-00cu","title":"mol-theme-generate: Custom name pool generation via cognition","description":"Currently polecat name themes are hardcoded in namepool.go. Simple theme selection is fine as static config, but custom name generation should be a molecule.

Examples requiring cognition:
- "Generate 50 polecat names in the style of [book/movie/game]"
- "Create names that match the project's domain (medical, financial, etc.)"
- "Extend the mad-max theme with 20 more obscure characters"

## Molecule: theme-generate
Generate custom polecat names for a rig.

## Step: analyze-request
Understand the naming request:
- Theme style or source material
- Number of names needed
- Any constraints (length, format)

## Step: generate-candidates
Generate candidate names matching the style.
Aim for 2x the requested count.

## Step: validate-names
Filter candidates:
- No duplicates with existing themes
- Appropriate length (3-15 chars)
- No problematic terms

## Step: create-theme
Write the custom theme to rig config or beads.
Register in name pool.
Needs: validate-names

## Notes
- Output is a new theme entry in BuiltinThemes equivalent
- Could be stored as a pinned bead for persistence","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:25:59.727107-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:25:59.727107-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-00cu","depends_on_id":"gt-3zw","type":"related","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:45.400818-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-01u","title":"Design: Collapse mail into beads","description":"## Proposal

Replace the separate mail system (JSONL inboxes) with beads issues using a naming convention.

## Rationale

If all state should be in beads (work items, swarm state, dependencies), why have a separate system for messages? Mail is just:
- Handoffs (agent → self)
- Commands (Mayor → Refinery)
- Escalations (Witness → Mayor)

These can all be beads issues with a special prefix.

## Design

### Convention
- Messages use `@-` prefix: `@-witness-1734012345`
- Assignee = recipient
- Status: open = unread, closed = read/acknowledged
- Priority 0 = urgent

### Commands (thin wrappers)
```bash
gt mail send witness -s "Subject" -m "Body"
  → bd create --prefix=@ --title="Subject" --assignee=witness --description="Body"

gt mail inbox
  → bd list --prefix=@ --assignee=$(gt whoami) --status=open

gt mail read @-abc
  → bd show @-abc && bd close @-abc
```

### Notification
Daemon watches for new `@-` issues and pokes relevant sessions.
Or: agents poll on heartbeat (simpler).

## What We Remove
- `mail/inbox.jsonl` files
- Mail JSONL read/write code
- Separate delivery mechanism

## What We Keep
- `gt mail` CLI (as wrapper)
- Handoff semantics
- Notification (via daemon or polling)

## Benefits
- One system, one sync, one query interface
- All communication in git history
- Simpler architecture

## Risks
- Beads prefix filtering must be efficient
- Namespace collision with user prefixes
- Performance for high-frequency messages (probably fine for handoffs)

## Decision Point
Do we need first-class mail support in beads (`bd mail` commands) or is convention sufficient?","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T02:10:18.32879-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T13:12:16.46526-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T13:12:16.46526-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-082","title":"Worker cleanup: Beads sync on shutdown","description":"Add beads sync verification to worker cleanup checklist and Witness verification.

## Update to Decommission Checklist (gt-sd6)

Add to pre-done verification:
- bd sync --status must show 'Up to date'
- git status .beads/ must show no changes

## Beads Edge Cases

Uncommitted beads changes:
  bd sync
  git add .beads/
  git commit -m 'beads: final sync'

Beads sync conflict (rare):
  git fetch origin main
  git checkout main -- .beads/
  bd sync --force
  git add .beads/
  git commit -m 'beads: resolve sync conflict'

## Update to Witness Verification (gt-f8v)

When capturing worker state:
  town capture <polecat> "bd sync --status && git status .beads/"

Check for:
- bd sync --status shows 'Up to date'
- git status .beads/ shows no changes

If beads not synced, nudge:
  WITNESS CHECK: Beads not synced. Run 'bd sync' then commit .beads/. Signal done when complete.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:47:21.757756-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:37.663168-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-082","depends_on_id":"gt-l3c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:47:35.977804-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-7o7","title":"Session pre-shutdown checks","description":"Session stop should verify clean state before killing, like PGT.

## Pre-Shutdown Checks

### 1. Git Working Tree Clean
```go
func checkGitClean(clonePath string) error {
    // git status --porcelain
    // Fail if any output
}
```

### 2. All Commits Pushed
```go
func checkCommitsPushed(clonePath string) error {
    // git log origin/HEAD..HEAD
    // Fail if any unpushed commits
}
```

### 3. Assigned Issues Handled
```go
func checkIssuesHandled(polecat *Polecat) error {
    // If polecat.Issue != "", check if closed or reassigned
}
```

### 4. Beads Synced
```go
func checkBeadsSynced(clonePath string) error {
    // bd sync --status in clone directory
}
```

## Behavior on Failure
1. First attempt: Nudge worker to fix
2. Retry up to 3 times with delay
3. After retries: Escalate to Witness/Mayor

## Integration
Modify internal/session/manager.go Stop():
```go
func (m *Manager) Stop(polecat string, force bool) error {
    if !force {
        if err := m.runPreShutdownChecks(polecat); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("pre-shutdown checks failed: %w", err)
        }
    }
    // existing stop logic
}
```

## Flags
- --force: Skip checks
- --grace-period N: Time to wait for fixes

## Dependencies
- Ties into gt-69l (hook system) - can be hook-based
- Ties into gt-f8v (Witness pre-kill verification)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stop fails if uncommitted changes (without --force)
- [ ] Stop fails if unpushed commits
- [ ] Clear error messages with fix instructions
- [ ] --force bypasses all checks","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:55.968983-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:05:02.795812-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-7oow","title":"gt mail: Cross-level routing is broken","description":"**Problem:**
When Mayor (at ~/gt) sends mail to a rig worker (gastown/crew/max), the message lands in town-level beads (~/.beads/) but the recipient checks rig-level beads (crew/max/.beads/).

**Reproduction:**
```bash
cd ~/gt
gt mail send gastown/crew/max -s 'Test' -m 'body'
# Message goes to ~/gt/.beads/ with prefix hq-*

cd ~/gt/gastown/crew/max  
gt mail inbox
# Does NOT see the message - it's looking in crew/max/.beads/
```

**Root cause:**
`findBeadsWorkDir()` walks up from CWD to find .beads. `Router.Send()` runs `bd create` in that directory. This means messages always go to the sender's beads, not the recipient's.

**Fix options:**
1. Route based on recipient address - if sending to rig/*, use that rig's .beads
2. Use a single shared beads database for all mail (simpler but less isolated)
3. Teach agents to check both levels (workaround, not fix)

**Related:**
- gt-ngu1: Pinned beads sorting (done but pointless if mail doesn't route)
- This blocks all cross-level mail functionality","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:57:30.258991-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T18:35:54.390121-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T18:35:54.390121-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-7we","title":"Swarms of One: Lightweight single-worker task dispatch","description":"Design and implement a lightweight pattern for firing off single workers to handle tasks without full swarm overhead.

## Context

Currently we have:
- town spawn: Creates a polecat in a rig with issue assignment
- Swarms (sw-*): Full lifecycle tracking with manifest, state, events, reports
- Ephemeral rigs (rig-*): Temporary worker groups for swarms

What's missing: A simple way to say 'fire off a worker to do X' without swarm ceremony.

## Design Questions

1. Should this be a new command like 'gt fire' or an option on existing commands?
2. Should single tasks still get swarm IDs (sw-N) for consistency/queryability?
3. Should it default to creating new workers or support --reuse for idle polecats?
4. How does this relate to crew workers (gt-cik)?

## Possible Interface

gt fire --rig gastown --issue gt-xyz [--prompt '...']
gt fire gastown/QuickTask --issue gt-xyz

## Related

- gt-cik: Overseer Crew (user-managed persistent workspaces)
- gt-kmn: Swarm System epic","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T16:51:02.716629-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:18.589027-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:18.589027-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-82y","title":"Design: Swarm shutdown and worker cleanup","description":"Design for graceful swarm shutdown, worker cleanup, and session cycling.

## Key Decisions

1. Pre-kill verification uses model intelligence (not framework rules)
2. Witness can request restart when context filling (mail self, exit)
3. Mayor NOT involved in per-worker cleanup (Witness responsibility)
4. Clear responsibility boundaries between Mayor/Witness/Polecat

## Subtasks (implementation)

- gt-sd6: Polecat decommission checklist prompting
- gt-f8v: Witness pre-kill verification protocol
- gt-eu9: Witness session cycling and handoff
- gt-gl2: Mayor vs Witness cleanup responsibilities

**Design complete.** Each subtask has full specification in its description.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:47:44.936374-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:49:22.849598-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T20:12:05.441911-08:00"}

@@ -179,7 +181,7 @@
 {"id":"gt-9nf","title":"gt spawn should create fresh polecat worktree, never reuse","description":"Currently gt spawn tries to reuse existing polecats, which causes:

1. Stale beads database
2. Stale code (behind main/integration)
3. Old inbox messages
4. Git history pollution

## Current Behavior

gt spawn:
1. Looks for idle polecat in pool
2. If found, reuses existing worktree
3. Assigns new issue

## Desired Behavior

gt spawn:
1. Always create FRESH polecat from current main/integration
2. Fresh worktree with clean beads
3. No reuse of old worktrees

## Name Pool Still Useful

Keep name pool for:
- Allocating themed names (mad-max, etc.)
- Tracking which names are in use

But worktrees should be created fresh each time.

## Implementation

In spawn.go, before starting work:
1. If worktree exists: remove it first
2. Create fresh worktree from integration branch
3. Sync beads from rig to polecat
4. Then proceed with work assignment

This ensures polecats always start with latest code and beads.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:23:35.250531-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:38:41.381329-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:38:41.381329-08:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in commit 82d718e","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-9nf","depends_on_id":"gt-8v8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:40:09.069331-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-a95","title":"Refinery background daemon mode","description":"Refinery currently only works in foreground mode. Background daemon is stubbed.

## Current State
manager.go line 128-129:
```go
// Background mode: spawn a new process
// For MVP, we just mark as running - actual daemon implementation in gt-ov2
return nil
```

## Requirements

### 1. Background Process Spawning
```go
func (m *Manager) Start(foreground bool) error {
    if !foreground {
        // Spawn gt refinery start --foreground as subprocess
        cmd := exec.Command(os.Args[0], "refinery", "start", m.rig.Name, "--foreground")
        cmd.Start()  // Don't wait
        // Record PID
    }
}
```

### 2. PID File Management
- Write PID to .gastown/refinery.pid
- Check PID validity on status
- Clean up stale PID files

### 3. Log Output
- Redirect stdout/stderr to .gastown/refinery.log
- Log rotation (optional for MVP)

### 4. Graceful Shutdown
- Handle SIGTERM/SIGINT
- Complete current merge before exit
- Update state to stopped

### 5. Health Check
- Process existence check via kill -0
- Optional: heartbeat file with timestamp

## Files to Modify
- internal/refinery/manager.go: Start(), Status(), process spawning

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] gt refinery start <rig> spawns background process
- [ ] gt refinery status shows running with PID
- [ ] gt refinery stop sends SIGTERM and waits
- [ ] Logs written to .gastown/refinery.log
- [ ] Survives terminal close","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:46:53.366619-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T15:24:39.124789-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T14:47:40.165105-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-a9y","title":"File locking for concurrent access","description":"Add file locking for concurrent access safety.

## At-Risk Files
- .gastown/swarms.json (or per-swarm state.json)
- .gastown/refinery.json
- polecats/<name>/state.json
- inbox.jsonl files

## Go File Locking
Use syscall.Flock for advisory locking:
```go
type FileLock struct {
    file *os.File
}

func AcquireLock(path string, timeout time.Duration) (*FileLock, error) {
    f, err := os.OpenFile(path+".lock", os.O_CREATE|os.O_RDWR, 0644)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    // Use syscall.Flock with timeout
}

func (l *FileLock) Release() error
```

## Integration Pattern
```go
func (m *Manager) saveState(ref *Refinery) error {
    lock, err := AcquireLock(m.stateFile(), 5*time.Second)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("could not acquire lock: %w", err)
    }
    defer lock.Release()
    
    // Read-modify-write cycle
}
```

## New Package
internal/filelock/
├── lock.go     # FileLock, AcquireLock
└── lock_test.go

## Apply To
- internal/refinery/manager.go: loadState/saveState
- internal/cmd/swarm.go: SwarmStore
- internal/mail/mailbox.go: Append, rewrite
- internal/polecat/manager.go: state operations

## Timeout Handling
Default 5 second timeout. Return error if lock not acquired.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lock files created (.lock extension)
- [ ] Timeout on lock contention
- [ ] All state files protected
- [ ] Locks released on error paths","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:15.641938-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:06:32.441426-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-dd8s","title":"gt molecule seed: create built-in molecules as beads","description":"The molecule infrastructure is complete but built-in molecules (engineer-in-box, quick-fix, research) need to be seeded into the beads database.

## Current State
- `gt molecule list` works but shows 0 molecules
- BuiltinMolecules() in internal/beads/builtin_molecules.go has 3 molecules defined
- No way to create them as beads

## Needed
Add `gt molecule seed` command that:
1. Reads BuiltinMolecules()
2. Creates each as a bead with type: molecule
3. Uses well-known IDs (mol-engineer-in-box, mol-quick-fix, mol-research)
4. Idempotent (skip if already exists)

## Acceptance Criteria
```
gt molecule seed
gt molecule list   # Shows 3 built-in molecules
```

+{"id":"gt-drbd","title":"Add no-PR instructions to mol-polecat-work at two points","description":"Update mol-polecat-work in builtin_molecules.go to explicitly forbid GitHub PRs.

## Two Points to Add Instructions

### 1. submit-work step
When polecat is ready to submit:
- Push branch to origin
- Create beads merge-request issue
- DO NOT use gh pr create or GitHub PRs

### 2. CLAUDE.md polecat context
Add to polecat role instructions:
- Never use gh pr create
- Never create GitHub pull requests
- The Refinery processes merges via beads MR issues

## Why Two Points
- Molecule step description guides the workflow
- CLAUDE.md reinforces at context level
- Belt and suspenders approach

## Implementation
1. Update PolecatWorkMolecule() submit-work step description
2. Update prompts/roles/polecat.md with explicit prohibition

 {"id":"gt-id36","title":"Deacon Kernel: event processing molecule architecture","description":"
Extend the Deacon from "health orchestrator" to "event processing kernel" that runs a 
molecule (rounds) each wake cycle, like an OS kernel scheduler.

## Core Insight

The Deacon already has:
- Wake cycle (daemon poke, timer, lifecycle request)
- Mail as event queue
- Health scanning
- Lifecycle processing

But it is REACTIVE. This epic makes it PROACTIVE with a structured molecule:

```
Deacon Wake
├── Step 0: Session Check (should I cycle myself?)
├── Step 1: Health Scan (Mayor, Witnesses, Crew, Refineries)
├── Step 2: Lifecycle Processing (cycle/restart/shutdown requests)
├── Step 3: Plugin Execution (scheduled, event-triggered, human-requested)
├── Step 4: Event Callbacks (timers, subscriptions)
└── Step 5: Heartbeat + Wait
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Session cycling first** - Token costs are quadratic with session length.
   Deacon should aggressively cycle to fresh sessions. First step of every
   wake is "do I need a new session?" based on context budget.

2. **Pinned bead with marching orders** - A pinned bead (like gt prime for
   agents) defines the Deacon's rounds molecule. This is the Deacon's
   "kernel" - its operating loop.

3. **Keepalive file for daemon quieting** - Instead of daemon tmux-poking
   the Deacon (which interrupts work), Deacon writes a keepalive file.
   Daemon reads it and backs off. Worst case: Deacon forgets, gets one
   extra heartbeat it squelches.

4. **Molecule-based rounds** - The wake cycle becomes a molecule with steps.
   Each step can have plugins (inline attention). Steps can be added/modified
   by updating the pinned bead.

## Architecture

```
Go Daemon (minimal, dumb)
├── Reads {townRoot}/deacon/keepalive.json
├── If fresh: do nothing
├── If stale: poke Deacon (fallback)
└── Process lifecycle requests (still daemon's job)

Deacon (Claude agent, smart)
├── Wakes: poke, timer, mail event
├── Writes keepalive (quiets daemon for duration of rounds)
├── Runs rounds molecule:
│   ├── session-check: cycle if context budget low
│   ├── health-scan: check agents, remediate
│   ├── lifecycle: process requests
│   ├── plugins: run scheduled/triggered plugins
│   ├── events: process timer/event callbacks
│   └── complete: update heartbeat, wait
└── Reads marching orders from pinned bead
```

## Relation to Existing Work

- Extends gt-5af (Deacon infrastructure) - CLOSED
- Relates to gt-axz (Plugin architecture) - P3
- Fits into phase3-deacon of gt-ngpz (Christmas Plan)
- Enables gt-976 (Crew lifecycle) as part of health-scan step

 {"id":"gt-qsvq","title":"gt doctor: Detect and clean up orphaned sessions/processes","description":"## Problem
Orphaned tmux sessions and Claude processes accumulate over time, consuming memory.

## Discovered Orphans
- gt-deacon: Idle Claude session, not part of any rig

## Proposed Solution

### gt doctor --check (default)
Detect issues without fixing:
- Orphan sessions (not matching rig/polecat/crew/witness/refinery/mayor pattern)
- Claude processes without parent tmux session
- Tmux sessions without Claude (stuck at bash prompt)
- Polecats marked 'working' but session idle

### gt doctor --fix
Clean up detected issues:
- Kill orphan sessions
- Kill orphan Claude processes
- Optionally reset stuck polecat state

### gt gc (alternative name)
Short alias for cleanup operations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects orphan sessions
- [ ] Detects orphan processes
- [ ] Safe cleanup (doesn't kill active work)
- [ ] Reports what was cleaned","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T01:06:39.046959-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T07:54:51.210265-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T07:54:51.210265-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-rana","title":"Patrol System: Agent lifecycle loops with attachments","description":"Enable Gas Town agents to run continuous patrols, survive crashes, and hand off work across sessions.

## Core Concepts
- **Attachments**: Molecules bound to agent's pinned bead until complete
- **Patrols**: Cyclic molecules that loop (deacon, witness, refinery)
- **Quiescent**: Agents that sleep until triggered (witness, refinery)

## Design Doc
See docs/patrol-system-design.md

## Phases
Phase 1: Foundation (attachment field, daemon detection, mol-deacon-patrol)
Phase 2: Quiescent Agents (wake triggers, witness/refinery patrols)
Phase 3: Callbacks and Plugins (mail protocol, plugin runner)
Phase 4: Polish (gt patrol status, metrics, tuning)

## Key Decisions
- Attachment as field on pinned bead (not edge type, for now)
- Mail-based orchestration for all callbacks
- Queue replacement for heartbeat delivery (not pile-up)
- Burn and respawn for patrol loops (not in-place reset)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:23.416949-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:23.416949-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-rana.1","title":"Phase 1.1: Attachment field on pinned beads","description":"Add attached_molecule field to pinned bead schema.

## Schema Change
Add to handoff/pinned bead issues:
- attached_molecule: string (root issue ID of attached mol)
- attached_at: timestamp

## Implementation
- Update beads Issue struct if needed
- Or: use labels/metadata field
- Ensure bd show displays attachment info

## Acceptance
- Can set/clear attachment on a bead
- bd show displays attachment status","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:34.251531-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:51:01.902014-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:51:01.902014-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented attachment fields: AttachMolecule, DetachMolecule, GetAttachment APIs","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rana.1","depends_on_id":"gt-rana","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:34.253378-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-rana.2","title":"Phase 1.2: Daemon attachment detection","description":"Daemon polls Deacon's pinned bead to detect naked state.

## Implementation
- Daemon knows Deacon's pinned bead ID
- Every heartbeat cycle: bd show <pinned> --json
- Parse attached_molecule field
- If null/empty: Deacon is naked

## Actions on Naked
- Spawn mol-deacon-patrol
- Attach to Deacon's pinned
- Nudge Deacon to start

## Failsafes
- Keepalive file monitoring
- Stale detection (>10min no progress)
- Force nudge on stale

```bash

```

## Context
The fart joke: instantiating a proto can produce either:
- A Mol (solid/substantial output)
- A Wisp (gas/ephemeral output)

## Implementation
- Add 'fart' as an alias in the mol subcommand
